design_philosophy.core_loop: Alternate between helicopter flight missions (navigation, combat, stealth landing) and ground infiltration missions (item collection, stealth, puzzle-solving with security cards)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing stealth infiltration against detection risk; managing inventory of specialized tools (papers for deception, gas for incapacitation, explosives for sabotage) while solving mission-specific objectives
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite operative 'Johnny Jimbo Baby McGibbits' piloting an Airwolf-like helicopter to covert military bases, then conducting solo ground operations to stop a mad leader's forces
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid design forcing mastery of two distinct gameplay modes (flight sim + stealth action); mission structure alternates between these pillars; early stealth game emphasizing deception (fake papers) and non-lethal takedowns (gas) over combat; tongue-in-cheek tone in documentation

difficulty.continues_system: Four-letter passcode system allows players to return to completed missions in subsequent playthroughs
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpoint system; completion of each mission grants passcode for replay access
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; players must master both helicopter piloting (ADF navigation, friend-or-foe identification, stealth landing in whisper mode) and ground infiltration mechanics (item management, guard evasion, mine detection) as mandatory challenge gates

progression.unlock_mechanism: Six missions (three helicopter + three ground pairs) unlock sequentially; security card collection within each ground mission gates access to final objectives; mission-specific tools become available as needed (explosives only in final mission)
replayability_hooks: Passcode system enables direct mission replay without replaying earlier content
Multiple rooms in buildings require exploration to locate security cards
Varying mission objectives (nerve gas neutralization in mission 2, scientist rescue in mission 4, seven missile room destruction in mission 6) prevent rote repetition

world_building_techniques: Character naming and personality (Johnny 'Jimbo Baby' McGibbits establishes spy-thriller tone)
Mission briefings embedded in objective descriptions
Environmental detail (enemy bases, chemical labs, missile control rooms suggest Cold War espionage setting)
Tongue-in-cheek documentation tone (per reviews) creates humorous narrative layer

faction_indicators: Friend-or-foe radio identification system in helicopter missions creates us-vs-them dynamic
Mad leader as central antagonist defines opposition faction
False papers mechanic implies player infiltration of structured enemy hierarchy
danger_signals: Guards becoming suspicious triggers gas canister/grenade escalation response
Mine fields signal high-risk military installations
Whisper mode requirement for stealth helicopter landing indicates constant detection threat
10-minute timer in final mission creates time-pressure danger signal
environmental_storytelling: Multi-room buildings structure implies enemy base organization and defensibility
Security card placement in cabinets suggests administrative control systems
Separate helicopter/ground mission pairs create distinct operational zone narratives
Chemical lab presence and nerve gas vats establish biological weapons context
